hardware.name: Hardware
hardware.description: Consumer and creative-tech hardware that shifted what artists could build

web-standards.name: Web Standards
web-standards.description: Browser APIs and graphics standards that enabled new web-native creative work

ai-ml.name: AI / ML
ai-ml.description: Machine learning models, releases, and tools that opened new creative territory

artworks.name: Landmark Artworks
artworks.description: Notable works that defined or expanded what creative coding could be

institutions.name: Institutions & Communities
institutions.description: Schools, festivals, and communities that shaped the field

events.title: iPhone launches
events.description: First mass-market multitouch device. Reshaped expectations for interactive, gestural interfaces.

events.title: iPad launches
events.description: Brought multitouch to a canvas-sized device; widely adopted for sketching, performance, and installation control surfaces.

events.title: Microsoft Kinect (Xbox 360)
events.description: Consumer depth camera. Hacks within hours; OpenKinect drivers within weeks; an entire era of installation work followed.

events.title: Official Kinect SDK for Windows
events.description: Microsoft releases an official SDK after the OpenKinect community led the way.

events.title: Leap Motion ships
events.description: Sub-millimeter hand tracking in a desktop peripheral. Used in countless gestural installations and prototypes.

events.title: Oculus Rift DK1 ships
events.description: First widely available developer kit for modern consumer VR. Kickstarted the second VR wave.

events.title: Oculus Rift CV1 / HTC Vive (consumer VR)
events.description: Consumer VR ships. Vive adds room-scale tracking. VR becomes a viable medium for artists.

events.title: Meta Quest 2
events.description: Standalone, untethered VR at a consumer price. Made VR work radically more shippable.

events.title: Apple Vision Pro
events.description: Apple's first spatial computing device. Reframes XR around passthrough and eye/hand input.

events.title: WebGL 1.0 ratified
events.description: Khronos releases WebGL 1.0 spec. GPU-accelerated graphics in the browser becomes a baseline assumption.

events.title: WebRTC enters browsers
events.description: Peer-to-peer audio/video/data in the browser. Enables networked performance and telepresence work.

events.title: WebAssembly 1.0
events.description: Near-native code execution in browsers. Ports of C/C++ creative tooling become feasible.

events.title: WebGL 2.0 ships
events.description: OpenGL ES 3.0-equivalent in the browser. Transform feedback, instanced rendering, more.

events.title: WebXR Device API
events.description: Standard browser API for VR/AR content. Makes browser-based immersive work portable across headsets.

events.title: WebGPU ships in Chrome
events.description: Modern GPU API (compute + graphics) in the browser. Successor to WebGL.

events.title: TensorFlow open-sourced
events.description: Google releases TensorFlow. ML moves from research papers into reach of practitioners.

events.title: PyTorch released
events.description: Facebook AI Research releases PyTorch. Becomes the de facto ML research framework.

events.title: StyleGAN released
events.description: NVIDIA's StyleGAN. Convincing synthetic faces; a flood of generative art followed.

events.title: DALL·E announced
events.description: OpenAI shows text-to-image generation at quality that surprised even ML researchers.

events.title: Midjourney public beta
events.description: Discord-based text-to-image generator opens publicly. Mainstream creative use of generative AI begins.

events.title: Stable Diffusion released
events.description: Open-weights text-to-image model. Generative AI becomes runnable on a laptop GPU.

events.title: ChatGPT released
events.description: Conversational LLM hits 100M users in two months. Reshapes the entire creative-tech conversation.

events.title: GPT-4 released
events.description: Multimodal model with substantially stronger reasoning. Becomes a building block for creative tools.

events.title: OpenAI Sora announced
events.description: Text-to-video at minute-long durations with surprising temporal coherence.

events.title: Camille Utterback & Romy Achituv — Text Rain
events.description: Falling letters interact with the silhouette of viewers via real-time video. A canonical interactive installation.

events.title: Daniel Rozin — Wooden Mirror
events.description: Hundreds of motorized wooden tiles reflect viewers as a low-resolution image. Defined an aesthetic of physical pixels.

events.title: Aaron Koblin — Flight Patterns
events.description: FAA flight data rendered as luminous lines. An early viral example of creative coding meeting data.

events.title: Random International — Rain Room
events.description: Walk through rain that stops where your body is. Installation work goes mainstream museum.

events.title: Janet Echelman & Aaron Koblin — Unnumbered Sparks
events.description: Massive suspended sculpture painted in real time by audience phones via Chrome and WebGL.

events.title: Memo Akten — Learning to See
events.description: Real-time pix2pix translates everyday objects into landscapes/clouds/fire. An early landmark of artistic ML.

events.title: teamLab Borderless opens (Tokyo)
events.description: Permanent immersive digital-art museum. Set a global reference point for room-scale generative installations.

events.title: Refik Anadol — Machine Hallucinations
events.description: Large-scale GAN-driven projections on architecture. Brought generative ML art to mainstream institutions.

events.title: ITP at NYU founded
events.description: NYU's Interactive Telecommunications Program. Long-running incubator for creative technologists.

events.title: MIT Media Lab opens
events.description: Founded by Nicholas Negroponte and Jerome Wiesner. Many creative coding tools and ideas trace through here.

events.title: Gray Area founded (San Francisco)
events.description: Nonprofit fusing art and technology; long-running creative code education and a key US community hub.

events.title: Eyeo Festival begins
events.description: Annual Minneapolis festival convening artists, designers, and technologists. Defined a generation's creative-code conversation.

events.title: Resonate Festival begins (Belgrade)
events.description: European counterpart to Eyeo; key gathering for digital culture and creative practice.

events.title: School for Poetic Computation
events.description: Artist-run school in NYC for code as a creative and critical practice.
